author: nero_eth
content:
I feel similar.
Today we have 4s for propagation (of a big object) AND execution. At least 1s is wasted in timing games (popular relays [delay bid delivery](https://bloxroute.com/pulse/understand-and-win-the-ethereum-validator-rewards-game/) for their validators/customers to max out profits), sometimes even 2-2.5s.

With ePBS, the size of the block decreases by 80-90%, and it doesn't need to be executed/validated by the EL.
Intuitively, 2s should be possible if proposers broadcast at 0 or a few ms later.

author: tbenr
content:
spec question:

new attestation rule:
```
[IGNORE] When aggregate.data.index == 1 (payload present for a past block), the corresponding execution payload for block has been seen
```

I believe (at least this is what we do in teku) the rule can be stricter here: instead of "seen" wouldn't it better to be "fully imported" (with DA as well)?
We are talking about attestation voting for payload for the prior slot, so everithing should be fully imported by then.

WDYT?

author: tuyen4818
content:
agree with this
it should be the same to voting for a block where it's required to have the block in forkchoice or the attestation is ignored
